text:
Just couple questions regard planned upgrade. Currently have POS GF2MX400 and search card for about 100-150 Aussie Dollars. I've done bit research and have found few cards drawn attention the likes being Radeon 9550, Radeon 9550 Extreme and few from the 9600 series. I'm steering clear nVidia for that end the market they seem get blasted ATI.
current frame mind Get the 9550 for <$100 will 100% better than current card and have regrets, spend the rest the money something else.
But looking some reviews the 9550 Extreme seems pretty sweet aswell. has clockspeed 400/500 from the 9550 clock speed 250/400. has got few FPS the stock 9600 some benches.
But costs extra $50. Are the internals the same these cards, would able get the same figures from stock 9550 with aftermarket cooler?
Haven't read much about the 9550, but looking the specs the card, it's clearly worse than 9600, but doesn't cost much less. can't say anything about the 9550 Extreme, though. any case, they all support Direct 9.0 adn have similar speeds, don't think you'll too dissapointed with whatever you choose.
the other hand, would worth get some more money for something from the Radeon 9700/9800 series. The 9800 Pro probably the best bang-for-buck card there, but any them will much better than 9600 9550 because they are all 256-bit. The 9600 basically the top the low-end range cards, "upgrading" one isn't really much upgrade.
Most those R9550's that are less than $100aus will likely only have 64-bit memory access stay clear them and get one that has full 128-bit memory access.
Sorry but can't really help you that subject.
Yawgm0th believe would absolutely love out and buy 9800 Pro but simply can't/won't. don't have the cash, and even did I'd prefer spend elsewhere.
One more question, how well nonpro 9600 overclock?
remember correctly, Sapphire 9600 non-pro got somewhere around 400/560 OEM desktop with case fans... But again, might not remember that right.
But wouldn't get too worked over it. All the overclocking the world isn't going make 9600 play any modern game well. It's okay you're still into some the older Quake-3 based games, guess.
appears the same this, which 128-bit.
